title: Bacterial heat-shock transformation (NEB)
description: Chemical heat-shock transformation of competent E. coli (42 C / 30 sec) per the NEB high-efficiency protocol, with an electroporation note.

payload.body:
## Purpose

Introduce plasmid DNA into chemically competent E. coli by a 42 C heat shock, then recover and plate on selective medium.

## Materials

| Item | Amount per reaction |
| --- | --- |
| Chemically competent E. coli (e.g. NEB 5-alpha or DH5alpha) | 50 uL |
| Plasmid DNA (1 pg-100 ng) | 1-5 uL |
| SOC outgrowth medium (room temperature) | 950 uL |
| Selection plates (antibiotic), pre-warmed to 37 C | 1-2 |

## Steps

1. Thaw competent cells on ice until the last ice crystals disappear. Pipette 50 uL into a tube on ice.
2. Add 1-5 uL DNA (1 pg-100 ng). Flick to mix 4-5 times. Do not vortex.
3. Place on ice 30 min. Do not mix.
4. Heat shock at exactly 42 C for exactly 30 sec. Do not mix.
5. Place on ice 5 min.
6. Add 950 uL room-temperature SOC.
7. Recover at 37 C for 60 min, shaking at 250 rpm.
8. Warm selection plates to 37 C. Spread 50-100 uL per plate (do 10-fold serial dilutions in SOC for high-efficiency DNA). Incubate overnight at 37 C.

## Electroporation variant

For electrocompetent cells (e.g. NEB 5-alpha Electrocompetent, C2989): thaw on ice about 10 min, add 1 uL DNA to 25 uL cells in a chilled cuvette, pulse on your electroporator (NEB cites 1.7 kV, 200 ohm, 25 uF), immediately add 975 uL of 37 C SOC, then recover at 37 C for 60 min at 250 rpm. Salt in the DNA causes arcing, so use desalted or diluted DNA.

## Expected result

Colonies on the selective plates. NEB 5-alpha gives about 1-3 x 10^9 cfu/ug with pUC19. The no-DNA control plate should be blank.

## Notes and troubleshooting

- Heat-shock timing is critical; longer than 30 sec lowers efficiency.
- Highest efficiency is in the 100 pg-1 ng range; use less DNA, not more.
- Store competent cells at -80 C. Storage at -20 C significantly lowers efficiency.

## Safety

BSL-1 E. coli. Use sterile technique and autoclave cultures and plates before disposal.

## Source

Adapted from the NEB High Efficiency Transformation Protocol (C2987); electroporation note from NEB 5-alpha Electrocompetent E. coli (C2989).
https://www.neb.com/en-us/protocols/high-efficiency-transformation-protocol-c2987
